title: SQL Stored Procedures Quiz
description: Test your fundamental knowledge about stored procedures and their role in advanced SQL development.

questions.question: What is a 'Stored Procedure' in a SQL database?
options: A way to backup the database.
A prepared SQL code that you can save and reuse over and over again, similar to a function.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: Which of these is the primary benefit of using a Stored Procedure from an application server (like Node.js)?
options: Reduced network traffic since only the procedure name and parameters are sent.
It automatically generates a backup of the data.
It's a requirement of all CSS frameworks.

questions.question: Which SQL command is used to execute a stored procedure?
What's a common security benefit of using Stored Procedures?
options: Preventing SQL Injection and allowing users to perform operations without having direct access to underlying tables.

questions.question: When should you generally NOT use stored procedures?
options: For complex business logic that you want to keep in your application's Git repository for easier versioning and testing.
